title: Ep 02. Clarity on Training for Women with Dr Lauren Colenso-Semple
summary: In this episode, weвҖҷre joined byВ Dr. Lauren Colenso-Semple, a leading researcher whose work has reshaped our understanding of female performance across the menstrual cycle. title: Why Your Hormone Blood Tests Say You're Fine (But You Feel Like S*it)
summary: YouвҖҷve been told your hormone blood tests are вҖңnormal,вҖқ but you still feel exhausted, bloated, moody, or just plain off. So... what gives? In this solo episode, IвҖҷm breaking down exactly why conventional blood testing often fails to give women real answers about their hormone health. If youвҖҷve ever walked out of a doctorвҖҷs office with results that donвҖҷt match how you actually feel, this conversation is for you. IвҖҷll walk you through the common gaps in standard testing, how reference ranges are outdated, and why your symptoms matter more than the numbers on a lab report. ItвҖҷs time to stop settling for confusion. You deserve better answers, and it starts with asking the right questions, and knowing what kind of testing will really support your healing. Key Takeaways Your symptoms speak louder than your labs вҖ“ How you feel is always more important than what the paper says. Standard blood tests miss key hormones вҖ“ Many GPs only order basic panels, skipping critical markers like progesterone, T3, and thyroid antibodies. Outdated reference ranges distort results вҖ“ Most labs still use old ranges, especially for thyroid, leaving many women misdiagnosed or ignored. You can ask for better testing вҖ“ Functional ranges and cycle-aware hormone testing reveal far more than generic bloodwork. Test timing makes or breaks results вҖ“ Day 21 or 7 days post-ovulation is ideal for sex hormone testing; thyroid should be tested consistently at the same time. Invest in the right eyes вҖ“ Independent labs and expert interpretation are often the only way to get real clarity about your hormones. title: Mikayla Matthews: Finding Her Voice
summary: TW: Sexual Abuse In this weekвҖҷs episode, Tay is back for another girl-talk conversation with The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives star Mikayla Matthews! Mikayla bravely opens up about growing up in a chaotic home as one of seven siblings, navigating childhood trauma, and the sexual abuse she endured starting at age six. She shares how the aftermath of that trauma still affects her marriage today and the therapy, tools, and self-awareness that have helped her begin to heal. Mikayla touches on her thoughts on fellow The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives star Whitney LeavittвҖҷs appearance on the podcast, then shares what sheвҖҷs most proud of in herself: becoming the person she needed when she was young. title: How to Stop Letting Labels Define You, with Rio Ferdinand
summary: ThereвҖҷs a certain kind of strength that comes from knowing who you are, even when the world tries to tell you otherwise. ThatвҖҷs what stood out in my conversation with Rio Ferdinand. Not just the success, but the mindset behind it: the habit of refusing to be reduced to a single story. RioвҖҷs journey is a lesson in identity, drive, and turning fear into fuel. With all the labels the world tries to throw at us, itвҖҷs not just about ignoring them, itвҖҷs about choosing your own. From the football pitch to the ballet studio, Rio shows us what it means to challenge assumptions and walk your own path, even when itвҖҷs uncomfortable. In this episode, I also dig into a powerful psychological study that reveals how quickly our judgments are shaped by context, how one label can change everything we think we see. ItвҖҷs a sobering insight into how easily we box others inвҖҰ and how often we do it to ourselves. If youвҖҷve ever questioned the limits placed on you, or wanted to rewrite the script entirely, thereвҖҷs something in this one for you. Here is a list of studies referenced in the episode: Stereotype and Social Class Study. Researchers: John Darley and Paget Gross (1983)The Pygmalion Effect Study. Researchers: Robert Rosenthal and Lenore Jacobson (1968)Name Bias in Psychiatric Diagnosis. Researcher: Dr. Luke Birmingham (referenced in "Pigeonholed" by Laura L. Osterhout) Listen to the full episode with Rio Ferdinand here: https://pod.fo/e/15914

title: Matt Cogin - From Drug Dealer to International Leadership Coach - Episode 5
summary: In this powerful episode, IвҖҷm joined by international speaker and leadership coach Matt Cogin, whose story is a wild, raw, and deeply inspiring ride. Matt opens up about his early yearsвҖ”leaving home at 14, dealing drugs in high school, a near-fatal motorbike crash, and an overdose that became a wake-up call. His path to healing was anything but linear, but it lit the fire for a complete transformation. He shares the turning points that helped him shift his identity, the role communication plays in creating change, and what it really takes to leadвҖ”with presence, humility, and emotional intelligence. We also dive into fatherhood and raising emotionally resilient, confident kids in a world that doesnвҖҷt always reward vulnerability. Whether youвҖҷre navigating your own transformation, leading others, or raising the next generationвҖ”this episode is full of grounded wisdom, stories, and strategies to help you grow.

title: Sharyn Casey - The Diagnosis That Changed Everything

summary: In this episode, Olympic triathlete Hayden Wilde shares aВ powerful 2025 update. title: What is interoception, and how mood can affect how well your vaccine works
summary: This week weвҖҷre talking about interoception. That's the way the brain interprets the many signals that come from the body.  How useful is this skill, can you perfect it вҖ“ and when might it have a more negative effect?   In the studio with Claudia is health psychologist Professor Kavita Vedhara who explains why watching 15 minutes of the Two Ronnies, some Fawlty Towers and a bit of Elvis Presley, could boost how well your vaccine works.    And Claudia meets a group from Essex who are finalists in this yearвҖҷs All in the Mind awards.  The group teaches women who are experiencing anxiety, depression or abusive relationships how to ride a bike. WeвҖҷll hear how this simple community initiative is connecting people and even transforming lives, and why they just canвҖҷt stop smiling. The awards take place at the BBC Radio Theatre in London on 18 June and there is a ballot for free tickets which closes at midday on 31 May. You can enter online by going to www.bbc.co.uk/showsandtours/shows. title: The Telepathy Tapes and the Power of the Unspoken
summary: In this episode,В Gwyneth sits down with filmmaker Ky Dickens, creator ofВ The Telepathy Tapes, the podcast thatвҖҷs sparked a powerful conversation about connection, consciousness, and how we listen. Dickens shares what sheвҖҷs uncovered after years spent with families of nonspeaking autistic childrenвҖ”many of whom seem to communicate in ways that challenge everything we think we know about language, intuition, and the mind. They talk about the resistance to what we canвҖҷt quantify, the power of deep maternal bonds, and what becomes possible when we learn to truly listen. Whether you approach this with skepticism or curiosity, this conversation invites a new way of thinking about consciousnessвҖ”and the unexpected forms it might take. title: Should I worry about microplastics?
summary: Welcome to WhatвҖҷs Up Docs?, the podcast where doctors and identical twins Chris and Xand van Tulleken explore every aspect of our health and wellbeing.  In this episode, they delve into the issue of microplastics - minute fragments of plastic material that have been found in every environment on the planet, as well as in our brains and bodies. How did they get there, are they doing us harm and, if they are, what can we do about it? They speak to Environmental Toxicologist Dr Stephanie Wright from Imperial College, London about the evidence behind the dramatic headlines and why trying to measure the impact of microplastic on health is a challenge.  If you want to get in touch, you can email us at whatsupdocs@bbc.co.uk or WhatsApp us on 08000 665 123. title: How To Recover Quicker From LifeвҖҷs Setbacks | Annalie Howling
summary: Setbacks are an inevitable part of life, so what tools are most effective in getting us back on our feet and, perhaps, even stronger than we were before? Annalie Howling is a performance coach who works with leaders in business, elite sport and the Armed Forces, and who has personal experience in recovering from lifeвҖҷs challenges. She explains how a back to basics approach can help, the role of radical self-belief, why helping others can transform your own problems and the importance of finding a renewed purpose in life. In this episode, we have an honest and important conversation about mental health. If you or someone you know is struggling, you are not aloneвҖ”support is available; you can contact the Samaritans at: https://www.samaritans.org/ Annalie HowlingвҖҷs new book вҖҳUnapologetic: Shackle Your Shame, Reclaim Your PowerвҖҷ is out now. We love performance, which is why weвҖҷve launched ainslie + ainslie вҖ“ the first supplement brand to be developed inside elite sport. Now available for everyone. Find out more at www.ainslieainslie.com  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Why YouвҖҷre Aging Faster Than You Think & How to Slow It

title: Miriam Margolyes on Ageing, Onions, and Matters of the Heart
summary: BAFTA-winning actress Miriam Margolyes joins Dr Oscar Duke for the first appointment of the Bedside Manners podcast. Describing a childhood growing up as the daughter of a GP, Miriam shares how passing wind and earwax were common dinner-time topics. But as someone who has reached a considerable level of fame and following in later life, staying fit and healthy has taken on a new meaningвҖ”as she describes enduring knee replacement surgery and the TAVI (Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation) procedure on her heart. She also shares thoughts on the importance of maintaining social connections as you get older and "forcing your mouth to go up at the sides". Plus, Oscar reveals a potential diagnosis relating to the gastric effect of onions. Professor Michael Fertleman from HCA Healthcare UK (a specialist in geriatric medicine and orthopaedic rehabilitation) also joins Oscar to discuss the intricacies of elderly care, the risks and benefits of surgeries for older adults, and the future of care enhanced by technology. This episode is sponsored by HCA Healthcare UK.

title: How Do We Manage... Staying True To Our Values?
summary: In this episode of How Do We Manage Stephanie is joined in the studio by a global fashion icon.В Elizabeth von der Goltz has been a leading name in the fashion industry for decades. She worked her way up from a temp job at Barneys in New York to take on leadership roles at Bergdorf Goodman and Net-a-Porter and later became Chief Merchandising Officer for Matches.com and CEO of Browns. SheвҖҷs now working as a Luxury Fashion & E-Commerce Executive, Board Leader & Strategic Advisor. В  Elizabeth sits down with Stephanie to share her story of drive and self-belief, how she refused to participate in the more toxic elements of the industry, her own вҖңDevil Wears PradaвҖқ moment, and how it feels when things donвҖҷt go to plan at the top of your game. SheвҖҷs also got great advice for anyone just starting their career вҖ“ plus tips on how to stay true to yourself in both your approach to your work, and your own personal style. ABOUT THIS CONVERSATION:  In this episode, I speak with Cheryl, who brings insider experience from the pharmaceutical world and a deeply analytical mind to everything thatвҖҷs unfolded over the past few years. We dive into the role of the MHRA, the UKвҖҷs drug regulator, and explore whether itвҖҷs truly serving public health or merely rubber-stamping products handed to it by pharmaceutical companies. Cheryl shares her concerns about the lack of transparency, the failures of pharmacovigilance, and how freedom of information requests have revealed troubling gaps in regulatory oversight, especially in relation to the COVID-19 vaccine rollout. We talk about mRNA technology, gene therapy, and the disturbing trend of pushing products to market without adequate long-term safety data. Cheryl also highlights the yellow card system, supposedly in place to track adverse effects, which is shockingly underused and almost unknown to most doctors. See my substack for more information. title: Liver Alert: Recognizing the Subtle Signals of Trouble
summary: Your liver plays a critical role in your overall health, but signs of trouble can be easy to miss. In this episode, Nurse Doza uncovers the early warning signals of liver dysfunction, from chronic fatigue to weight gain, and explains how your diet, sleep, and stress levels impact liver health. Learn practical steps to support your liver naturally and optimize your well-being. 5 KEY TAKEAWAYS Persistent fatigue is often a sign of liver dysfunction, as the liver regulates energy metabolism. Poor sleep quality, including sleep apnea, is linked to an increased risk of liver disease. Gut health and liver function are closely connectedвҖ”irregular bowel movements may indicate liver issues. Cognitive decline and brain fog can be early indicators of non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD). Weight gain and metabolic imbalances are often tied to liver health and insulin resistance. title: Big Spaces, Small Hearts
summary: Hi Sleepy.  Tonight we stand together on the edge of something that maybe was never there to begin with. I talk about the third arm that vanished, the cliff that never was, and the Pope I almost licked.  We float through cathedral ceilings and asphalt plains, childhood fears and ABBA-induced tears, trying not to fallвҖ”or at least not fall alone.  ThereвҖҷs Harriet, singing in the car. ThereвҖҷs my dad, holding a band-aid like a book title. And thereвҖҷs you, Sleepy, drifting beside me in the strange safety of storytelling. Loss isnвҖҷt just absence. ItвҖҷs surprise. Sleep Tight! title: Damon Hill: Carrying His Father's Legacy Through F1вҖҷs Most Ruthless Era (E352)
summary: Damon Hill is a former Formula 1 World Champion and one of the most thoughtful voices in motorsport. The son of legendary racer Graham Hill, Damon carved out his own legacy in a sport shaped by speed, risk, and legacy. In this episode, he opens up with a rare mix of honesty and introspection, sharing stories that go far beyond the racetrack. The conversation dives into DamonвҖҷs memories of Ayrton Senna, not just as a rival, but as a human being. He reflects on SennaвҖҷs deep sense of duty to Brazil, his kindness off the track, and the emotional shockwave that followed his tragic accident. ItвҖҷs a raw, moving look at what it meant to lose someone who carried so much weight for an entire nation. Damon also gets personal about the influence of his father, Graham Hill. He talks about growing up in the shadow of greatness, navigating grief, and eventually finding his own path in the same high-stakes world.В  This episode offers a heartfelt exploration of the human side of racing, with Damon sharing honest lessons on family, legacy, and overcoming adversity that will resonate with fans and newcomers alike. title: Ep 3: 45% of Dementia Cases Could Be Prevented: Do These 14 Things Now
summary: рҹ§  Cutting your risk of dementia starts when youвҖҷre young. The action you take now is crucial. And the best part? What you do to stave off dementia will make your brain run optimally throughout life. Want to be smarter, have more energy and boost your memory? This is what you have to do. вҡЎ A 2024 report by the Lancet Commission found that 45% of dementia cases could be prevented through lifestyle changes alone. They even listed the 14 things you can improve to make prevention possible - and this is your step-by-step guide through each of them. рҹ’Ў In this episode of The Dementia Perspective you will learn: вһЎпёҺ Why вҖңbrain workoutsвҖқ and lifelong education are so important in staving off dementia вһЎпёҺ Exactly how the food you eat impacts your brain health - and how to optimise it вһЎпёҺ The true impact of loneliness and isolation - and how to save a life рҹҺҷпёҸ A podcast from the Guernsey AlzheimerвҖҷs Association - your essential guide to brain health, now and for the future. summary: рҹ§  What is it like to actually live with dementia? How do you navigate life through such a difficult time? title: Ep 1: What Dementia Really Is - And Why Memory Loss IsnвҖҷt Inevitable
summary: рҹ§  Your memory doesnвҖҷt need to get worse as you age; вҖңCognition is a lifelong processвҖқ. Brain health is on everyoneвҖҷs mind at the moment - and if youвҖҷre keen to keep yours operating at full capacity, this is for you. вҡЎ Dementia is on the rise, with cases expected to soar by 61% over the next few decades. Fortunately we understand the science of dementia and brain health better than ever before. The world is waking up to the impact of dementia on society - and now itвҖҷs time to do something about it. рҹ’Ў In this episode of The Dementia Perspective you will learn: вһЎпёҺ The difference between AlzheimerвҖҷs and dementia, and the many forms it can take вһЎпёҺ Just how much genetics influences dementia risk - and why thereвҖҷs always hope вһЎпёҺ How hormones and many other factors may cause dementia-like symptoms рҹҺҷпёҸ A podcast from the Guernsey AlzheimerвҖҷs Association - your essential guide to brain health, now and for the future. title: The Mind Full Podcast Live with Eric Lalor & Willa White
summary: For this extra special bonus-length live episode, IвҖҷm joined by Comedians and Actors Willa White and Eric Lalor. The lads sit down with me on stage at the fantastic PГәca Festival in Trim, Co. Meath to talk about their very unique experiences with mental struggles, the power of friendship, life lessons and a home birth that went very very weird! Willa White is living proof that you can come back from anything вҖ” with a bit of reflection, a lot of humour, and the kind of honesty that stops you in your tracks. HeвҖҷs battled addiction, transformed his life, and now uses laughter and his award-winning acting chopsвҖ” not just as punchlines, but as a path to healing. His hugely successful and powerful one man show the Examination has played in theatres across the world and, in recent times, he sold out both the 3Olympia Theatre and The Gaiety Theatre (the first comedian to do so in over 10 years), making him the first Irish man to play the venue since it was built back in 1939. Eric is a multi award-winning comedian and his latest one man show, LolвҖҷer By Nature was a sell out success at DublinвҖҷs Liberty Hall Theatre. You also might know him as the loveable rogue Cathal Spillane from Fair City but off-screen and stage heвҖҷs one of the funniest, soundest, and most razor-sharp comedians in the country. Check out one of WillaвҖҷs Work in Progress shows on the 18th & 19th June in the Axis Theatre in Ballymun, in the National Stadium in Dublin on Aug 2nd, or on Oct 1st in the TLT, Drogheda. Make sure you tune into the ladsвҖҷ award-winning You Must Be JokinвҖҷ podcast and follow @ericlalor and @willawhitecomedy on Instagram. If you're new to the series, why not take the time to go back and catch up on the wonderful interviews that you may have missed! title: The Use of Psilocybin for End of Life Care and Why Meditation and Breathwork Work to Manage Stress with Psychologist Shanae O'Leary
summary: TodayвҖҷs episode is one that I have wanted to create since January, when mum was told there was no more treatment and my family and I had to dive deep into death. I know no one likes to talk about it, but I actually want to change the narrative and become more open about this topic. Grieving is the most common human experience, everyone will have to endure it at some point so understanding end of life care a little better has empowered me and really helped with the healing journey.В  On the podcast I have Shanae OвҖҷLeary, a registered psychologist who has a strong passion in palliative care treatments. Shanae has done a lot of work on trauma and is currently working with a team of health professional and palliative care specialists to make psilocybin assisted therapy accessible to people given a terminal diagnosis who are experiencing existential distress towards the end of their life.В  More information about End Wise below: https://www.endwise.au/

title: Seasonal Affective Disorder (SAD) Season? HereвҖҷs Your Nutritional Survival Kit
summary: In this Mini Mikkipedia episode, Mikki dives into practical, evidence-based strategies to support your mood and energy during the darker months. Seasonal Affective Disorder (SAD) is more common than you thinkвҖ”and it's not just about feeling a little вҖңmeh.вҖқ With shorter days and less sunlight disrupting our serotonin and melatonin balance, Mikki explains why we crave carbs, struggle with low mood, and experience poor sleep. She unpacks the nutritional tools that can make a real difference, from key supplements like creatine and vitamin D, to protein and gut health support. Whether you experience SAD or just feel off in winter, this episode is packed with doable, science-backed tips to help lift your mood and energy. title: Episode 272 Karen Coleman -Ostrov Five years living an Intermittent Fasting Lifestyle and Never Looking back.
summary: Karen is 65 years old and has tried justabout every diet you can think of вҖ“ The Scarsdale Diet, Atkins and keto diets, Weight Watchers, Noom, the Cabbage Soup, Healthy Inspirations, grapefruit diet, Whole30, Slim-Fast, South Beach, Green Smoothie Diet, Nutrisystem, hCG вҖ“ if youвҖҷve heard of it, Karen probably tried it!В В   Some of these diets brought Karen temporarysuccess, and others caused her to gain weight or slow her metabolism.В  None of them were sustainable, and the weightalways crept back. One day in the summer of 2020, Karen was perusing Facebook and saw a photo of her sister-in-law, Dedra Campbell.В  Karen thought Dedra had posted a вҖңThrowback ThursdayвҖқ photo, as her sister-in-law looked young, slim, and glowing.В  Karen was shocked to learn the photo was recent, so she immediately called her sister-in-law to find out what she did to lose weight and seemingly age backwards.В  Dedra told her the secret was IntermittentFasting brought up negative thoughts and feelings in Karen.В  In KarenвҖҷs mind, fasting = deprivation and hunger.В  However, with her sister-in-lawвҖҷs encouragement, she listened to the basics of the program and bought the book she recommended, вҖңFast.Feast.Repeat.вҖқ by Gin Stephens.В  Karen read the book and said she would try it for one day to see how it went.В  On August 13, 2020, Karen did a clean 18/6 fast, found it to be effortless, and the rest, as they say, is history.В   Karen was invited to GraemeвҖҷs group, The Fasting Highway, by Dedra after another IF guru closed her Facebook group and moved to another platform.В  Karen had been fasting for not quite a year when she joined The Fasting Highway.В  Karen had only lost 10 pounds by this time, but was encouraged and inspired by Graeme, his book, and fellow group members to stay the course and trust the process. Karen realised that her weight loss journey was her own, and she could not compare herself to others. Karen is 5вҖҷ5вҖқ and her highest weight ever was 208 pounds.В  She could not bend over to tie her shoes at that weight and was always out of breath.В  She also suffered from sleep apnea.В  KarenвҖҷs IF starting weight was 175.4 pounds, and she set her goal weight at 160 pounds (because she didnвҖҷt think that at herage, being post-menopausal, having T2D, and being insulin resistant, she could get below that weight).В  However,on July 3, 2023, Karen weighed 149 pounds, a weight she hadnвҖҷt seen in almost 20 years.В  Best thing вҖ“ there wasno struggle to get there!В  Yes, it did take almost 3 years to lose the weight, but the point is вҖ“ she lost it withintermittent fasting when nothing else worked!В  ItвҖҷs now been almost 5 years since Karen started intermittent fasting, and her current weight is 142 pounds.В  She sometimes gets as low as 138 pounds, bouncing between that number and 142 pounds.В  Karen went from a size 12 (US) to wearing sizes 4 and 6.В  Intermittent fasting gave Karen theconfidence that she would never have to lose weight again вҖ“ and for the first time in years, she doesnвҖҷt have 5 different clothing sizes in her closet.В  She donated all her вҖңbigвҖқ clothes to charity, knowing sheвҖҷd never need them again.В   Another personal win is that Karen decidedto embrace her greys and, as a result, cut all the hair colour out.В  She would never have had theconfidence to do this at her higher weights! To join the Patreon Community.  Please go to www.patreon.com/thefastinghighway or visit the website www.thefastinghighway.com for more information. To book a one-on-one support session with Graeme Please go to the website, click " get help" and " get coaching " to book a time . title: Episode 214: What can we do to prevent bowel cancer in young people?
summary: Can Gut Health in Childhood Help Prevent Bowel Cancer Later in Life? This week on the podcast, weвҖҷre talking about something that might feel a little confronting but also incredibly empowering: how our kidsвҖҷ health today could shape their future health tomorrow. Bowel cancer rates are on the rise in young people, and new research is pointing to a surprising contributor вҖ” one weвҖҷve been talking about for a long time here at Natural Super Kids: gut health.В In this episode, we will break down what this means for parents without fear, but with practical action in mind. HereвҖҷs what we cover: What the latest research says about childhood gut bacteria and bowel cancer risk.How early-life factors like C-section births, antibiotics, and diet can impact long-term gut health.Subtle signs that your childвҖҷs gut may be out of balance вҖ” from tummy troubles to mood changes.Easy, gut-supportive changes you can start making right now (even if life feels busy).рҹҺ§ Tune in to learn how small steps like adding variety to meals or spending more time outside can make a big difference to your childвҖҷs health, both now and in the years to come. Feeling unsure where to begin? В If youвҖҷre ready to take action but donвҖҷt know where to start вҖ” I created the Six Steps to Better Gut Health for Kids course just for you. рҹ’ӣ Click here to learn more and enrol вҖ” and start supporting your childвҖҷs gut health in a way that truly works for your lifestyle. Curious to learn more about the research I mentioned in this episode? If you're interested in diving a little deeper, here are a couple of easy-to-read articles that explain the science behind the gut healthвҖ“bowel cancer connection in more detail: рҹ”— Read this article on The Conversation  рҹ”— Or this one from Get Surrey Don't forget to visit Natural Super Kids to find more resources for you and your child's health.В  This episode is proudly sponsored by my membership, the Natural Super Kids Klub. title: More Than Footy: Paddy McCartin on Owning Type 1 and Its Biggest Life Lessons
summary: When Paddy McCartin was diagnosed with type 1 diabetes at just eight years old, his world shiftedвҖ”but his passion for footy never wavered. Despite ongoing battles with diabetes and a career-ending series of concussions, Paddy carved out a remarkable AFL journey that took him from Geelong to the Sydney Swans and back into the hearts of the diabetes community. In this episode, Paddy opens up about the highs and lows of elite sport with type 1, how technology transformed his management, and the power of letting people in. From the locker rooms to life after retirement, this is Paddy like youвҖҷve never heard him beforeвҖ”raw, reflective, and real. HereвҖҷs what weвҖҷre covering: -The early signs of type 1 and how an 8-year-old learned to self-manage with stubborn independence -Why finger pricks were PaddyвҖҷs most hated partвҖ”and how he eventually overcame that barrier -The вҖңcarrotвҖқ that got him back on a pump and chasing his AFL dream -The real reason he kept his diabetes quiet in footy circlesвҖ”and the moment he finally opened up -How everything changed with a single speech to the Swans playing group -Game day routines, insulin strategies and the tech hacks that helped him thrive on the field -The bittersweet reality of early retirementвҖ”and how he found purpose in giving back -What heвҖҷd say to his diabetes if it were a person (spoiler: itвҖҷs not all bad) вҖҰ and a whole lot more Chapters: вҸі [00:00] вҖңI Just DidnвҖҷt Want to Play FootyвҖқ: The Diagnosis That Changed Everything вҸі [01:59] Meet Paddy McCartin: Footy Star, Type 1 Role Model, Reluctant Retiree вҸі [07:10] First Symptoms, First Needles, and an 8-Year-OldвҖҷs Determination вҸі [13:05] Rebel with a Cause: Fighting Against Diabetes Burnout вҸі [19:10] Back on the Pump: The Deal Paddy Made to Chase the AFL Dream вҸі [25:22] Hidden Fears and Public Perception: Why Paddy Stayed Quiet вҸі [28:07] The Swans, the Speech, and the Shift in Identity вҸі [34:16] From Draft Night to Diabetes Tech: How It All Played Out вҸі [36:13] Game Day Strategy: Insulin, Oats, Temp Targets and Gels вҸі [45:11] Playing with Tech: Pumps on the Sideline, Physios with Phones вҸі [50:11] Post-Match Highs, Hypos and Recovery Routines вҸі [55:21] Sydney Swans and a Second Chance: The Comeback Chapter вҸі [58:30] Forced Retirement, Lasting Gratitude, and a Whole Lot of Perspective вҸі [01:04:41] Pilates, Weights and Wiser Habits: How Paddy Moves Today вҸі [01:10:14] Diet, Fasting and Feeling Good in 2025 вҸі [01:16:08] The Magic Wand Question: Would He Cure His Diabetes? вҸі [01:20:10] The One Lesson HeвҖҷd Pass on to Every Young Type 1 Hope you enjoy! title: Floored! Episode 76 with Dr Sara Chan Reardon
summary: Hello & Welcome to today's episode where I had the great pleasure of chatting with my friend & colleague, Dr Sara Chan Reardon, about her new book, Floored! (out June 10th) рҹҘірҹҘірҹҘі Sara has built not only a successful clinical career but also a huge social media presence delivering pelvic health education, and now sheвҖҷs in her writer era - her new book Floored! is now available for pre-order (with some extra goodies if you sign up via thevagwhisperer.com In our conversation рҹ“қWe discussed the challenges of transitioning from a clinical background to writing and the importance of educating women about all aspects of their pelvic health at every life stage, from menstrual health through perinatal to menopause and beyond, including bladder, bowel and pain issues рҹ“қSara also emphasised the need for a comprehensive approach that includes both in-person therapy and online resources. She shared her experiences as a pelvic health pysiotherapist, highlighting the challenges of reaching patients and the importance of demystifying pelvic health issues.  рҹ“қShe also discussed the challenges of writing a book on the topic, including balancing technical information with accessibility for a wider audience & the conversation also touched on the importance of empowering women to advocate for themselves in medical settings and the need for a more preventative approach to pelvic health. рҹ“қIвҖҷve had the pleasure reading an advance copy of SaraвҖҷs book вҖҳFlooredвҖҷ and I think itвҖҷs going to be so valuable to both women who are interested in learning more about pelvic health at every age & stage but also to clinicians who are looking for accessible language and strategies to educate and empower women  Pre-order your copy today at your local bookshop! (Floored will be available for purchase on June 10th) рҹ“ҡ рҹ“ҡ рҹ“ҡ  Want to learn more about promoting sexual health, treating dyspareunia, understanding the effects of different types of contraception and our evidence based role in promoting fertility, including ART/IVF? Then you might be interested in my latest online course, Female Sexual Health, Contraception & Fertility - you can find all the details at the links in my bio or visit CelebrateMuliebrity. com  #celebratemuliebrity рҹҘірҹҘірҹҘі

title: A Lighter Body, A Louder Voice: What We Gained by Losing almost 200lbs [S2E8]
podcast_name: The Dose with Paddy and Belinda
summary: We lost almost 100 pounds eachвҖ”but spoiler alert: we didnвҖҷt magically become perfect humans. In this laugh-through-the-tears episode, Paddy and Belinda unpack the wild, wonderful, and downright weird things that happen after weight loss. From squeezing into Paul Mescal-style OвҖҷNeills shorts to Belinda strutting around in mustard mini skirts (with a zip, obviously), we share whatвҖҷs changedвҖ”from food guilt to fashion fitsвҖ”and what still hits us hard (hello, body image baggage and funeral rosary anxiety). Paddy revisits a letter he once read on The Biggest Loser and reflects on the pressure to be вҖңfixed.вҖқ Belinda speaks about showing up for herself and finally not caring if she ever steps on a scale again. We talk about protecting our peace, ditching cold shoulder tops, and finding joy in a full-fat ice cream with syrup and a flakeвҖ”without spiralling into self-hate. This episode is full of big laughs, deep sighs, awkward honesty, and hard-won freedom. If youвҖҷve ever felt less-than, tried to shrink yourself, or wondered what it might feel like to finally take up spaceвҖ”this oneвҖҷs for you. P.S. WeвҖҷre not fixed. But weвҖҷre f***ing free. ======= If you enjoy this podcast please follow / subscribe, download, and leave us a review.В  If you don't enjoy this podcast............ well this is awks.В  Combined Paddy & Belindas weight loss totals over 190lbs supported by GLP1 Medications Ozempic & Mounjaro along with a focus on nutrition, movement and associated lifestyle changes. None of the content in this episode is to be treated as medical advice or promotion towards any specific treatment or medication - this is a sharing of a personal journey. Always seek out personal support from your GP / Medical Care team before beginning or changing any aspect of your healthcare. We are not medically qualifiedВ  - simply sharing our insights based on our journeys so far. title: Why menopause shouldn't hold you back from your exercise goals with Cathy Duffy
summary: Francesca and Louise have been trail running for several years now, and while they love it as a hobby, both know how menopause and ageing can have an impact on getting into exercise and maintaining it.  So this week, they are talking to Cathy Duffy, a running coach with Mile 27 in Australia, and who also has a Bachelor of Human Movement Science. She is focused on making sure women feel safe and comfortable when it comes to exercise, not only to achieve their goals, but to train with consideration of hormonal impacts during the various stages of their lives. title: Joe Canning : Straight shooter

summary: Topics covered : Hurling career, All Ireland Finals (2017 & 2018), growing up, learning from failure, resilience, criticism, coping with fame, love, loss, proposal story, becoming a Dad. This week, IвҖҷm joined by All-Ireland winning hurler and businessman Joe Canning. HeвҖҷs a proud Galway man who now lives in Limerick with his wife Meg and their baby daughter Josie. In the first part of our conversation, we dive into his hurling career вҖ” focusing on the highs and lows of All-Ireland finals, especially those unforgettable matches in 2017 and 2018. В  Later, we move into more personal territory. Joe opens up about family вҖ” the loss of his much-loved Mam, proposing to Meg, and what fatherhood means to him. Naturally more introverted as a person, heвҖҷs honest about how he sees the world вҖ” from having no time for people-pleasing to how he turns criticism into motivation. title: Jeff Innocent: Love, Lurchers & Late-Life Epiphanies
podcast_name: How To Be 60 with Kaye Adams
summary: On this weekвҖҷs episode, weвҖҷre joined by stand-up legend Jeff Innocent, who proves that life doesnвҖҷt mellow you вҖ” it just sharpens the comedy. From farting in first class to falling in love at 68, Jeff takes us on a ride through what it really means to age disgracefully (and brilliantly). He opens up about his unexpected daughter, his late-life fame thanks to a young comicвҖҷs intervention, and why heвҖҷs only just getting honest on stage вҖ” and in life. Whether youвҖҷre mortified by mid-train mishaps or still working out how to be yourself in your 60s, this oneвҖҷs for you. Because no one tells you the best bits of life might still be round the corner вҖ” even after a cabbage curry. Get in touch with your thoughts at podcast@htb60.com.  Hosted on Acast. title: EP 82: Embracing the Fitness to Say Yes
podcast_name: The Hybrid Lab with Dr. Alyssa Olenick
summary: In this episode of The Hybrid Lab, Dr. Alyssa Olenick shares her philosophy on fitness, emphasizing the importance of having the physical capacity to say yes to life's adventures. пјҲе·Іиҙӯе…ҘзүҲжқғпјү зү№еҲ«еЈ°жҳҺпјҡеҳүе®ҫеңЁиҠӮзӣ®дёӯзҡ„еҸ‘иЁҖд»…д»ЈиЎЁдёӘдәәи§ӮзӮ№гҖӮ -иҒҠеӨ©зҡ„дәә- дё»жҢҒпјҡиЁҖдҝЎShayne aka иҖҒиҸңзӢ—дҝЎе“Ҙ е…ЁиҒҢи·‘жӯҘеҶ…е®№еҲӣдҪңиҖ…пјҢдёҡдҪҷи·‘жӯҘзҲұеҘҪиҖ…пјҲе…Ёй©¬PB2е°Ҹж—¶38еҲҶ29з§’пјү еҳүе®ҫпјҡжқҺзҠҒJames Li иҖҗе…ӢеӨ§дёӯеҚҺеҢәи·‘жӯҘе“Ғзұ»жҖ»ж•ҷз»ғгҖҒеүҚдёҠжө·жҳ“еұ…й©¬жӢүжқҫдҝұд№җйғЁжҖ»ж•ҷз»ғ гҖҒеүҚдёӯеӣҪеӣҪе®¶з”°еҫ„йҳҹдёӯй•ҝи·‘йЎ№зӣ®дё»ж•ҷз»ғгҖҒеӣҪйҷ…з”°иҒ”и®ӨиҜҒдё»ж•ҷз»ғгҖҒеҸҢж–ҷдё–з•ҢеҶ еҶӣдјҜзәіеҫ·В·жӢүеҠ зү№пјҲBernard title: Rock Bottom to Redemption with Niall Harbison
summary: Send us a text "What if your darkest moment became the catalyst for your greatest transformation?" This question lies at the heart of my profound conversation with Niall Harbison, whose remarkable journey from corporate success to rock-bottom alcoholism to purposeful revival might just change your perspective on what truly matters in life. Niall's story begins as a private chef for billionaires aboard luxury yachts, where million-dollar fuel bills and private jets delivering Italian cheese were commonplace. He witnessed firsthand the hollow nature of extreme wealth before returning to Dublin to build his own successful businesses. Yet beneath this veneer of achievement lurked depression, anxiety, and a growing dependence on alcohol. When COVID struck, Niall's life spiraled into a six-week bender in Thailand that nearly killed him. Lying in a hospital bed, certain he was dying, he experienced a revelation that would transform everything: the corporate success, social media followers, and material possessions he'd chased meant absolutely nothing. What flashed through his mind instead were simple moments of childhood joy and connection. That rock-bottom moment became what Niall now calls "the best thing in my life." Upon recovery, he discovered purpose in rescuing Thailand's street dogs. What began as feeding a few hungry canines has expanded into a charity that feeds 1,200 dogs daily, performs thousands of sterilizations monthly, and has built a hospital named after Tina, a special rescue who lived just six months in freedom after a lifetime of abuse. Niall's transformation at age 41 stands as powerful testament that it's never too late to reinvent yourself. His advice resonates with brutal honesty: "Follow your own path. Take risks. You don't have to do the life that's mapped out for you." Whether you're questioning your purpose or just need a reminder of what truly matters, this conversation might be exactly what you need to hear. Subscribe now and join the thousands finding inspiration in these authentic human stories of transformation. Thanks for listening! You can watch the full episode on YouTube here. DonвҖҷt forget to follow The Laura Dowling Experience podcast on Instagram @lauradowlingexperience for updates and more information. You can also follow our host, Laura Dowling, @fabulouspharmacist for more insights and tips. If you enjoyed this episode, please subscribe and leave a reviewвҖ”it really helps us out! Stay tuned for more great conversations.

title: The Marathon Mindset вҖ“ Raising Children with T1D for the Long Run
summary: In this motivating and down-to-earth episode, Catherine explores what it means to parent a child with type 1 diabetes (T1D) with a marathon mindset rather than a sprint mentality. She challenges the pressures of achieving perfect blood sugar numbers and warns against the unsustainable, all-or-nothing approaches often fuelled by social media and fear. Instead, she offers a practical, compassionate framework using SMART goalsвҖ”Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, and TimelyвҖ”to encourage gradual, joyful, and sustainable diabetes management. Listeners are encouraged to shift the focus from perfection to progress, helping children learn patience, resilience, and flexibility. Key messages include: Diabetes is a lifelong journey, not a race to perfect numbers.All-or-nothing thinking can lead to guilt and burnout.SMART goals can support steady improvement without overwhelming children.Joy and balance matter вҖ” food and life should be celebrated, not restricted.Self-compassion and role-modelling are essential to raising empowered, healthy kids with T1D.CatherineвҖҷs closing message reminds parents that imperfect days are normalвҖ”and that raising a child with diabetes is not about getting everything right, but about staying in it for the long run with love, grace, and determination. The Diabetic Mammy is the heartfelt, no-nonsense podcast where type 1 diabetes meets motherhood, community, and a whole lot of real talk. Hosted by Catherine, a mammy navigating the highs and lows of type 1 diabetes, each episode blends personal insight with practical wisdom, offering support for parents, carers, and anyone living with or loving someone with diabetes. From tech transitions and hospital diagnoses to school lunches and stolen sleep, this podcast embraces the messy, meaningful moments that make up life with chronic illness. title: Ep 1: From Denial to Freedom: My 12-Year Journey with Hypothalamic Amenorrhea
summary: In the debut episode of the HERhythm Podcast, I am sharing my personal 12-year struggle with hypothalamic amenorrhea (HA), my battle with anorexia, and the rollercoaster journey to restoring my health, fertility, and the conception journey I was not prepared for. From being diagnosed with anorexia at age 15 to dealing with the aftermath of disordered eating, Type A pressure, and countless relapses, this episode is an honest look at the sometimes painful, yet empowering journey that brought me to where I am today. In this episode, I will cover: - The anorexia diagnosis at 15: How being hospitalized and struggling with eating disorders shaped my health journey. - The many relapses: The emotional and physical toll of falling back into disordered eating, and the deep Type A pressure to "do it all." - Hypothalamic Amenorrhea (HA): The impact of losing my period, struggling with body image, and eventually discovering HA. - The up-and-down fertility journey: How restoring my fertility was not a straight path and the moments of hope and frustration I faced. - Bone health struggles: Navigating a diagnosis of osteopenia and the importance of rebuilding bone strength as part of recovery. - Type A pressure: How perfectionism and a high-achieving mindset kept me stuck for so long, and how I finally learned to let go. Spoiler alert: The journey was not easy, and there were plenty of setbacks, but each challenge brought me one step closer to reclaiming my health and fertility. If you are on a similar path of recovery, I hope this episode brings you the hope and encouragement you need to keep going. I would love to connect with you! Feel free to reach out to me if you ever need support because it is now my goal to help other women along this journey too xox Find me over on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/herhythm_platform

title: Ep 4 - Is Creatine Overrated?
summary: In this episode of Front Page Fitness, Dr. Eric Trexler and Dr. Lauren Colenso-Semple discuss various health and nutrition topics, including the bizarre diet of a TikTok creator, the implications of low-carb diets, and a controversial paper on LDL cholesterol and heart disease. They also delve into the raw milk debate, the safety of pasteurization, and the efficacy of creatine supplementation, addressing common misconceptions and emphasizing the importance of evidence-based practices in nutrition and fitness. title: The Whole Truth About Supplements: Why Wholefoods Work When Synthetics DonвҖҷt вҖ“ with Sheridan Joy Austin
summary: LetвҖҷs talk about the supplement industryвҖ”and why so much of it is failing women. In this raw, uplifting and incredibly educational conversation, IвҖҷm joined by Sheridan Joy Austin, the beautiful mind and founder behind Foraged For You, to dive into what it really takes to nourish our bodiesвҖ”especially in pregnancy, postpartum, and perimenopause. Sheridan opens up about how her own unexpected pregnancy revealed the gaps in prenatal nutrition and how that led to the creation of her now cult-favourite wholefood blend, The MotherвҖҷs Blend. We talk liver, folate, iron, vitamin A, and why isolating nutrients in synthetic supplements often creates more harm than good. Sheridan shares her journey of bringing a product to life with true integrity, the resistance sheвҖҷs faced, and why she refuses to cut cornersвҖ”even when the wellness industry tells her to. This episode is for any woman whoвҖҷs ever questioned her supplements, felt overwhelmed by mixed health advice, or just wants to understand how to truly fuel her body with the wisdom of nature. Key Takeaways Why real food always winsвҖ”how nutrient-dense ingredients like liver and kelp naturally support pregnancy, postpartum, and hormone recovery better than synthetics. What your supplements arenвҖҷt telling youвҖ”why isolating nutrients like folic acid or iron can backfire without their critical co-factors like vitamin A, copper, and magnesium. How to read the fine printвҖ”what hidden fillers like maltodextrin and silicon dioxide are doing to your gut, and how to choose products with real integrity. Why iron deficiency might not be about ironвҖ”how symptoms like fatigue and poor immunity may be rooted in deeper imbalances most blood tests donвҖҷt show. The truth about vitamin A in pregnancyвҖ”why liver-based vitamin A isnвҖҷt the enemy, and what the research really says about birth defects and deficiency risks. Stephanie also unpacks why many common supplementsвҖ”like gummiesвҖ”may not be as effective as we think, and shares insights from her new book, The Longevity Nutrient, which simplifies complex science into actionable, everyday tools. If you're curious about cutting-edge health discoveries or looking to create a fuller, longer life without the overwhelm, this episode is a must-listen. title: Episode 45: Ivy League scientist Dr. Lisa Miller on consciousness, the brain & the nature of reality
summary: Dr. Lisa Miller is a psychologist and scientist from Columbia University, widely regarded as the leading authority on the science of spirituality. While science and spirituality are often seen as opposites, Dr. MillerвҖҷs groundbreaking research reveals that the two are deeply interconnected.  Her work has shown that we are actually biologically hardwired for spirituality. So itвҖҷs no surprise her contributions have earned critical acclaim amongst fellow scientists and the media. In 2014, Dr. MillerвҖҷs TED Talk went viral, racking up 2.6 million views, where she demonstrated how spirituality can act as a protective shield against depression. Since then, her work has been featured by Big Think, The New York Times, The Wall Street Journal, TIME, and NBC.  SheвҖҷs also authored two bestselling books вҖ“ The Spiritual Child and The Awakened Brain.  In this episode, we dive deep into the research, exploring how science has proven our spiritual nature. Dr. Miller unpacks some really fascinating findings, including the role of synchronicity, the connection between spirituality and a sense of purpose, and why having something greater than yourself in life is essential.  We also discuss practical ways to cultivate spiritual awarenessвҖ”both individually and then with other people. The beauty is that whether you consider yourself spiritual or not, this episode will open your eyes and unlock an entirely new way of looking at life. title: The Metrics: Blood Sugar Basics!
summary: In this gentle and empowering episode, CatherineвҖ”host of The Diabetic MammyвҖ”welcomes both new and seasoned parents of children with type 1 diabetes. Titled вҖңThe Metrics,вҖқ this episode is inspired by two mothers who, despite their different points on the diabetes journey, both revealed they had never been clearly taught what key diabetes numbers mean. This episode walks listeners through the essential metrics of diabetes management with warmth and clarity: Glycemic Control вҖ“ the overall aim of managing blood sugars in a safe range, with emphasis on balance over perfection.Blood Glucose Levels (BGL) вҖ“ explained in both mmol/L and mg/dL, with realistic goals tailored to each child.HbA1c вҖ“ a long-term measure of average blood sugars, usually targeted under 7%, but personalized.Time in Range (TIR) вҖ“ a newer CGM-based concept reflecting how often blood sugars stay in the target range. Catherine reassures listeners that understanding these numbers is a process and that it's okay not to have it all figured out at once. She emphasizes that good glycemic control supports physical health, energy, and emotional well-being, but also reminds parents that numbers arenвҖҷt everythingвҖ”their childвҖҷs joy, safety, and spirit matter just as much. She closes with an uplifting message of community, validation, and encouragement: You are doing a great job. YouвҖҷre not alone. And this isnвҖҷt a raceвҖ”itвҖҷs a relationship. The Diabetic Mammy is the heartfelt, no-nonsense podcast where type 1 diabetes meets motherhood, community, and a whole lot of real talk. Hosted by Catherine, a mammy navigating the highs and lows of type 1 diabetes, each episode blends personal insight with practical wisdom, offering support for parents, carers, and anyone living with or loving someone with diabetes. From tech transitions and hospital diagnoses to school lunches and stolen sleep, this podcast embraces the messy, meaningful moments that make up life with chronic illness. Whether youвҖҷre newly diagnosed or a seasoned warrior, The Diabetic Mammy is your space to feel seen, supported, and never alone. Connect with Catherine by email on catherine@abundantlydiabetes.com or reach her on Instagram: abundantlydiabetes  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: How to Stop Letting Labels Define You, with Rio Ferdinand
summary: Essential Habits is a new weekly series from High Performance. Hosted by Damian Hughes, each episode breaks down powerful habits from world-class performers and thinkers - so you can apply them in your own life. HereвҖҷs DamianвҖҷs take on the first episode, featuring Jo Malone:  "Sometimes life moves so fast we forget we even can stop. ThatвҖҷs something Jo reminded me of in this episode, the quiet power of pausing and taking stock before charging ahead. JoвҖҷs story is a masterclass in resilience and reinvention. With around 6,000 thoughts flying through our minds each day, many of them negative, itвҖҷs not just about thinking less, but choosing whatвҖҷs worth holding on to. Together, we explore how stopping to organise our thoughts and truly understand our story can turn moments of crisis into turning points. I also unpack a fascinating piece of neuroscience, a study with a less-than-glamorous title, but a powerful message, showing how our brains naturally prioritise rewarding memories. If youвҖҷve ever wanted to hold on to what matters and let the rest slip awayвҖҰ this oneвҖҷs for you." Here is a list of studies referenced in the episode: The Persistence and Transience of Memory. Authors: Blake A. Richards & Paul W. FranklandEbbinghaus's Forgetting Curve. Author: Hermann EbbinghausDiscovery of 'Thought WormsвҖҷ. summary: Save 20% on all Nuzest Products WORLDWIDE with the code MIKKIPEDIA at www.nuzest.co.nz, www.nuzest.com.au or www.nuzest.com This week on the podcast, Mikki speaks to Marco AltiniвҖ”data scientist, athlete, and founder of HRV4TrainingвҖ”about his personal and professional exploration into periodised nutrition. Known for his evidence-based insights into heart rate variability and training readiness, Marco has recently turned the lens inward, experimenting with how varying his fuelling strategies around training intensity and adaptation goals can impact performance and recovery. In this conversation, we dive into what led Marco to shift from a rigid, data-driven approach to one that embraces flexibility, intuition, and physiological feedback. We talk about the science behind carb periodisation, the mindset shifts required to break free from вҖҳhigh carb by default,вҖҷ and how this approach might offer not just better resultsвҖ”but a more sustainable relationship with training. Whether youвҖҷre a high-performing athlete or a time-poor weekend warrior, Marco shares insights that might just change the way you think about fuelling. Marco holds a PhD in applied machine learning, a M.Sc.В  in computer science engineering, and a M.Sc. n human movement sciences and high-performance coaching. He has published more than 50 papers and patents at the intersection between physiology, health, technology, and human performance. В Marco blends cutting-edge data analytics with practical insights for athletes and coaches. His work has been published in peer-reviewed journals, and heвҖҷs a frequent voice in the endurance and sports science communities. Through his own training and experimentation, Marco explores the integration of physiological dataвҖ”like HRV, resting heart rate, and glucose monitoringвҖ”with periodised nutrition and recovery strategies. title: It's Not All In Your Head with Clinical Psychologist Dr Bex Parkes
summary: In this raw, validating, and deeply insightful episode, weвҖҷre joined by the brilliant Dr Bex Parkes - clinical psychologist, founder of All About Wellbeing, and advisor to The Cyclist - for a conversation that pulls back the curtain on the powerful intersection between mental health and womenвҖҷs health. Bex shares her personal journey through years of undiagnosed fibroids and autoimmune challenges, and how learning to trust her body became a radical act of self-advocacy. Together we unpack the messy, often overlooked emotional side of chronic pain and womenвҖҷs health - from the impact of medical gaslighting to rebuilding trust with your own body when it feels like the system isnвҖҷt listening. This episode explores the science and lived realities behind mind-body pain cycles, trauma, central sensitization, and why the psychological toll of not being believed can sometimes hurt more than the symptoms themselves. Bex offers real-world tools for calming your nervous system, practicing radical acceptance, and changing the narrative around what it means to вҖңpush throughвҖқ versus honoring when your body needs rest. With honesty and a big dose of compassion, this conversation is a lifeline for anyone whoвҖҷs ever felt dismissed, confused, or alone in their health journey. Tune in for practical advice, myth-busting truths, and a reminder that your pain - and your experience - are real. This is a must-listen for anyone ready to rewrite the rules around healing, find community in the tough stuff, and learn how psychology can be a game-changer in both personal and collective health. title: Fierce Self Compassion: How We Harness Kindness to Speak Up and Thrive. With Kristin Neff.
summary: Welcome to another favorite encore edition of Untangle.  Kristin Neff is an extremely well-regarded expert and pioneer in the field of self-compassion. Her most recent book is called Fierce Self Compassion: How Women can Harness Kindness to Speak Up, Claim their Power and Thrive. She discusses how self-compassion allows us to be tender with ourselves in difficult situations, but also that it sometimes needs to be fierce вҖ” like the mama bear energy that comes from a place of love, service, and a desire to reduce harm. We must, she says, act courageously to protect ourselves from harm and injustice вҖ” and to motivate necessary change in ourselves and society. Her messageвҖҰwomen can be fierce and tender. Best quote from the book: "Tender Self-Compassion harnesses the energy of nurturing to alleviate suffering while fierce Self-Compassion harnesses the energy of action to alleviate suffering." We need both. As always, weвҖҷre Untangle, the podcast from Muse, the brain sensing headband dedicated to unlocking your brains potential.В  WeвҖҷre here, just like youвҖҰto learn, grow, share and connect. We hope you enjoy this episode. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

title: Is вҖҳare you ok?вҖҷ a good question and how your eyes give away memory precision
summary: Are you ok?   ItвҖҷs a question that might be at the front of your mind during a traumatic event. But how helpful is it and can it even make matters worse? We answer a very intriguing listener question.   And in the studio with Claudia is Catherine Loveday, Professor of Cognitive Neuroscience, whoвҖҷs excited about a study that has uncovered a fascinating way of measuring just how precise our memory is. Clue: ItвҖҷs all about the eyes.  We also reveal the latest finalist in the All in the Mind awards. This week it's Dr Rajeev Kumar who was nominated by Holly Batyka-Berry for his support through Holly's experiences of severe postnatal depression.    The awards take place at the BBC Radio Theatre in London on 18 June and there is a ballot for free tickets which closes at midday on 31 May. You can enter online by going to www.bbc.co.uk/showsandtours/shows. title: Гүpisode 88 : Le syndrome des ovaires polykystiques (SOPK)
summary: AujourdвҖҷhui, nous parlons dвҖҷun sujet qui touche des millions de femmes dans le monde, souvent sans mГӘme quвҖҷelles ne le sachent : le syndrome des ovaires polykystiques, plus connu sous le nom de SOPK. En particulier nous aborderons son lien avec la rГ©sistance Г  lвҖҷinsuline et ce quвҖҷil est possible de faire pour lвҖҷamГ©liorer ou le renverser.В  Statistiques sur le SOPK  Les critГЁres diagnostiques :  Anovulation chronique ou ovulation irrГ©guliГЁre HyperandrogГ©nie clinique ou biologique Ovaires polykystiques Г  lвҖҷГ©chographie Ce ne sont pas des kystes.  ProblГЁme de fertilitГ©, mais aussi dГ©sordre mГ©tabolique et hormonal complexe qui affecte la santГ© physique, mentale et Г©motionnelle des femmes qui en sont atteintes. Les phГ©notypes  Le SOPK ne se limite pas aux ovaires, et est Г©volutif.  La rГ©sistance Г  lвҖҷinsuline est lвҖҷun des mГ©canismes les plus courants associГ©s au SOPK, mais elle nвҖҷest pas seule en cause.  Les symptГҙmes courants du SOPK LвҖҷinsuline, la pierre angulaireВ  Le test de KraftPourquoi toutes les femmes qui ont une rГ©sistance Г  l'insuline n'ont pas toutes un SOPK?  Les approches thГ©rapeutiques naturelles et pharmacologiques Le jeГ»ne et le cycle menstruel  Les produits naturels qui peuvent aider  LвҖҷapproche pharmacologique : module les symptГҙmes CвҖҷest maintenant lвҖҷheure de lвҖҷastuce de la semaine :В il faut Г©couter l'Г©pisode pour la connaГ®tre  Les messages clГ©s de lвҖҷГ©pisode sont :В  Le SOPK est un syndrome systГ©mique, pas seulement un trouble ovarien.Il affecte plusieurs systГЁmes du corps (mГ©tabolique, hormonal, Г©motionnel) et ne se limite pas Г  la fertilitГ© ni Г  la prГ©sence de kystes sur les ovaires. La rГ©sistance Г  lвҖҷinsuline est un facteur central du SOPK.Elle concerne jusquвҖҷГ  70 % des femmes atteintes, parfois de faГ§on subtile, et alimente un cercle vicieux hormonal et inflammatoire aux consГ©quences multiples. Une prise en charge globale et personnalisГ©e est essentielle.Nutrition adaptГ©e, exercice musculaire, gestion du stress, bon sommeil et, au besoin, soutien pharmacologique ou naturel permettent dвҖҷagir sur les causes profondes plutГҙt que de masquer les symptГҙmes. Nous aimerions remercier chaleureusement la gynГ©cologue Martine Aubry pour sa relecture du prГ©sent Г©pisode et ses conseils judicieux.В  Pour en savoir plus sur le SOPK et son lien avec la rГ©sistance Г  lвҖҷinsuline et que vous lisez lвҖҷanglais, nous vous suggГ©rons le livre PCOS Plan de Nadia Pateguana, prГ©facГ© par Dr Jason Fung.В  www.cuisinemed.com HГ©bergГ© par Ausha. Visitez ausha.co/politique-de-confidentialite pour plus d'informations.

title: Emma Grede on Unapologetic Ambition
summary: In this episode of The goop Podcast, Gwyneth sits down with Emma GredeвҖ”the force behind some of fashionвҖҷs most influential and cultural shifting-brands. Grede shares her journey from East London to running the boardrooms of billion-dollar companies like Skims and Good AmericanвҖ”and how she built it all by rewriting the rules and trusting her instincts. They talk about what it takes to scale with conviction, the illusion of balance, and why letting go of perfection can sometimes be the most powerful move. ItвҖҷs a conversation about ambition, resilience, and carving a life thatвҖҷs entirely your own. title: How to have a difficult conversation
summary: Welcome to WhatвҖҷs Up Docs?, the podcast where doctors and identical twins Chris and Xand van Tulleken explore every aspect of our health and wellbeing.    How do you tell your friend that something theyвҖҷve been doing is really starting to hurt your feelings? Talk to your mum about her health? Or even ask your partner to try something new in bed? If just thinking about these types of conversations makes your stomach flip, youвҖҷre not alone. Most of us avoid moments like these, but how important is it to confront our interpersonal issues head on?  The docs are joined by Chartered Psychologist Kimberley Wilson to explore how we can better prepare for lifeвҖҷs tough but important conversations. Chris and Xand are committed to sharing their personal health and wellbeing journey on WhatвҖҷs up Docs, and in this episode they unexpectedly found themselves having a difficult conversation of their own. If youвҖҷd like to share your thoughts on difficult conversations or anything else that caught your attention in this episode - you can email us at whatsupdocs@bbc.co.uk or Whatsapp us on 08000 665123. title: Interview: Aging, menopause, and rethinking fashion for comfort in midlife | Stacy London
summary: вҖңMy lens around style doesnвҖҷt have anything to do with style anymore вҖ”В itвҖҷs about physicality,вҖқ says stylist and fashion consultant Stacy London. вҖңWhat do I want to be able to do? How do I keep myself strong?вҖқВ  StacyвҖҷs message has resonated for many women, and for this episode, she joins Dr. Shoshana Ungerleider, host of TED Health, at TED2025 for a special live conversation about why women are so embarrassed to talk about aging. For Stacy, aging became a chance to reassess her relationship to her body, and her experience with menopause and spinal surgery shifted her focus to health and wellbeing. title: Welcome to The Diabetic Mammy
summary: Warm, candid, empowering pilot episode, in which Catherine introduces The Diabetic MammyвҖ”a podcast dedicated to navigating the emotional, practical, and social complexities of living with type 1 diabetes, especially in the context of motherhood. Sharing her own journey from being diagnosed at age 4 to becoming a mother of two daughters (both also diagnosed with type 1 diabetes), Catherine invites listeners into a space of honesty, humour, and community. She explains her motivation for launching the podcast: to create a supportive environment for diabetic mammies who often juggle invisible challenges like burnout, mom guilt, and balancing self-care with parenting. Catherine emphasises that this podcast is not medical advice but a place of real talk, real stories, and shared experiences. Please connect with Catherine on Instagram @ abundantlydiabetes or email her  The Diabetic Mammy is the heartfelt, no-nonsense podcast where type 1 diabetes meets motherhood, community, and a whole lot of real talk. Hosted by Catherine, a mammy navigating the highs and lows of type 1 diabetes, each episode blends personal insight with practical wisdom, offering support for parents, carers, and anyone living with or loving someone with diabetes. From tech transitions and hospital diagnoses to school lunches and stolen sleep, this podcast embraces the messy, meaningful moments that make up life with chronic illness. Whether youвҖҷre newly diagnosed or a seasoned warrior, The Diabetic Mammy is your space to feel seen, supported, and never alone. Connect with Catherine by email on catherine@abundantlydiabetes.com or reach her on Instagram: abundantlydiabetes  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Simon Thomas | Coming Back From Anxiety and Grief to Take a Second Chance at Life
summary: Georgie welcomes her former Sky Sports screen husband, and now Soccer Saturday host, Simon Thomas, into the Performance People studio. Simon opens up about his journey through sports broadcasting, revealing the truth behind his big break on Blue Peter, explaining the personal challenges of overcoming anxiety and the pressures of live television, how the sudden death of his wife, Gemma, left him utterly broken and he shares the shaping forces that helped him rebuild his life again. He discusses how he handles pressure through preparation, the inside stories on what really happens in live TV, describes what itвҖҷs like to follow Jeff Stelling into one of live footballвҖҷs most demanding jobs and he gives Georgie a dressing down for misidentifying the Blue Peter ship. In this episode, we have an honest and important conversation about mental health, including experiences with suicidal thoughts. If you or someone you know is struggling, you are not aloneвҖ”support is available. If you are struggling with either, you can contact the Samaritans at: https://www.samaritans.org/ Simon is President of Blood Cancer UK. Since losing his wife, Gemma, suddenly to acute myeloid leukaemia in November 2017 he has been raising awareness of blood cancer symptoms ever since. For more information or to donate, go to: https://bloodcancer.org.uk/ We love performance, which is why weвҖҷve launched ainslie + ainslie вҖ“ the first supplement brand to be developed inside elite sport. Now available for everyone. Find out more at www.ainslieainslie.com The ainslie + ainslie Performance People podcast talks to high-performers in the world of sport and beyond, to bring defining moments, hard-earned insights and expert advice to everyday performance. title: How Do We Manage... Trauma?
summary: Today's guest is Dr Paul Conti.В Paul is a psychiatrist and author, specialising in trauma and the impact it can have on our minds and bodies. His 2022 bookВ Trauma: The Invisible Epidemic, comes with an endorsement from Lady Gaga, who claims he saved her life. In this episode, Paul and Stephanie talk in depth about trauma, both individual and collective, how we recognise it, and how we can work to heal from it. Paul emphasies the importance of placing equal value on our mental health as our physical health, and shares tips and advice on how to do that. They discuss the concept of inherited trauma, and what to do if you feel thatвҖҷs something affecting your life.В  And Paul also reminds us of how to take joy in the simple things, and find gratitude in the every day. Content warning: this episode contains discussions around mental health, suicide and trauma. title: How Different Diets Impact Your Health | Dr. Christopher Gardner
summary: My guest is Dr. Christopher Gardner, Ph.D., professor of medicine and director of nutrition studies at Stanford. He is known for his pioneering research on the impact of dietary interventions on weight loss and health. We compare ketogenic, vegetarian, vegan and omnivorous dietsвҖ”and why there is no one-size-fits-all approach. All agree, however, that eliminating or dramatically reducing processed foods is best for health. We discuss the protein needs controversy; plant vs. animal proteins; the importance of fiber and low-sugar fermented foods for gut health and inflammation; and how diet affects gene expression. We also review food allergiesвҖ”including gluten, wheat, dairy and soyвҖ”as well as raw dairy. The episode offers data-supported advice for healthier eating. summary: Hi, Sleepy. This is me again. Henrik.  Floating above the endless void of meaning and language and strangely shaped English sentences. We talk about parenting, loneliness, oceans, boats, triangles on Venus, the impossibility of controlling anything, and the maddening mystery of what birthdays felt like in 1348.  I admit my fears. I praise curiosity. I thank my imagination. And I speak, because thatвҖҷs what I do. Words tumble like socks in a dryer, some pairing off, some lost in the dark. This isnвҖҷt a lullaby. ItвҖҷs something else. Something that floats. Welcome to another introspective and imaginative journey to sleep. Sleep tight! title: Rescued with Niall Harbison
summary: My incredible guest this week has got a heart the size of a Great Dane, the determination of a Jack Russell, and the Instagram account thatвҖҷs made millions ugly cry before breakfast! Niall Harbison is a Tyrone man but later moved to Dublin where he became a chef with a career that saw him cooking for the likes of rock stars and business moguls often on luxury yachts! But beneath all of this lay troublesome deep seated mental health issues that culminated in chronic addiction and a traumatic breakdown in Thailand in 2020. NiallвҖҷs journey since then is nothing short of fascinating and inspiring. Niall dedicates his time to rescuing dogs and is the author of not one butВ twoВ wonderful books. First, there wasВ вҖҳ - how street dogs taught me the meaning of lifeвҖқ and nowвҖ”heвҖҷs back with a brand new bookВ вҖҳTina: The Dog Who Changed The WorldвҖҷ, a follow-up that proves our greatest teachers can appear in the unlikeliest places. And they may love tennis balls. In this lovely conversation youвҖҷll hear how the high-adrenaline chef culture enabled his addiction, what he identified as his 3 drinking triggers, the pressures of a global online following, the celebs whose hearts his work has touched and how special dogs Tina, Hank the Tank & Cindy Crawford all carry stories that mirror his own healing journey. If you're new to the series, why not take the time to go back and catch up on the wonderful interviews that you may have missed! Visit www.dermotwhelan.com for more information and don't forget my new book Busy and Wrecked is out now!  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: S12 E5 Managing menopausal hair loss
summary: This episode of the podcast focuses on a menopausal symptom that is very common but can be quite distressing to experience - female pattern hair loss. From an ever-widening hair part to temples that are creeping backwards, the good news is there is much to be done to halt the thinning. Our expert to guide us through the options is Chelcey Salinger IAT, a consultant trichologist at the Australian Trichology Centre, Sydney and Adelaide, (www.hairlossspecialist.com.au) who specialises in hair loss, baldness, alopecia areata, hair breakage, hair damage, psoriasis and other scalp problems in women and men.  Chelcey is the executive director of the International Association of Trichologists (USA). She is involved in the education of Trichologists, tutoring students all over the world and supervising the clinical training of students in Australia. To find a registered trichologist, visit www.iattrichology.com.  В  See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: Breast Cancer & Menopause: A SurvivorвҖҷs Raw Story from Struggling to Thriving
podcast_name: All About You With Dr Shauna Watts
summary: In this episode, Sonya Lovell, a breast cancer survivor and menopause advocate, shares her powerful story of navigating life after cancer. Diagnosed with breast cancer in her 40s, Sonya underwent chemotherapy and was thrust into medically induced menopause, leaving her unprepared and unsupported. She now uses her experience to advocate for better education and support for women dealing with menopause, especially those going through cancer treatment. In this episode, we discuss the challenges Sonya faced with hormone therapies like Tamoxifen and Letrozole, and how she took control of her health with strength training, hormone therapy, and lifestyle changes. title: Mini Mikkipedia - Mildly Elevated CRP? Here's What It Really Means
summary: In this episode of Mikkipedia, Mikki explores a commonly overlooked health markerвҖ”C-Reactive Protein (CRP). While traditional lab ranges often label values under 5 mg/L as "normal," Mikki explains why even mild elevations (around 3вҖ“4 mg/L) can signal underlying low-grade inflammation. She breaks down what CRP actually reflects, the various root causes (from gut issues and food sensitivities to overtraining and hormonal shifts), and what practical steps you can take to investigate and lower it. If your blood work seems вҖңfineвҖқ but youвҖҷre not feeling your best, this episode is for you. Tune in to learn: What CRP is and why вҖңnormalвҖқ might not be optimalCommon hidden triggers of low-grade inflammationHow to track and troubleshoot symptoms across lifestyle, diet, and hormonesThe role of food sensitivities, histamine, overtraining, and gut healthStrategic testing, supplements, and self-experimentation tips Contact Mikki:https://mikkiwilliden.com/https://www.facebook.com/mikkiwillidennutritionhttps://www.instagram.com/mikkiwilliden/https://linktr.ee/mikkiwilliden Save 20% on all Nuzest Products WORLDWIDE with the code MIKKI at www.nuzest.co.nz, www.nuzest.com.au or www.nuzest.comCurranz supplement: MIKKI saves you 25% at www.curranz.co.nz or www.curranz.co.ukВ off your first order

title: Episode 271 -Rhonda Douglas, Tweaking It until it was easy a four-and-a-half-year journey.
summary: My name is Rhonda Douglas.В  I am a wife, mother of three adult children and Nana of 4 grandchildren.В  I am a real estate broker and own a real estate company specialising in residential sales and property management.В I enjoy playing golf, walking, and watching movies in my spare time.В В  I have struggled with weight since my early 20s after my first child, gaining and losing over and over.В  I am now 53 years old and found IF in 2020, and started fasting in June 2020, and it has taken me 4 1/2 years to tweak it until it became easy. Our Patreon Supporters Community - An Invitation to Join Us! Please consider joining the Fasting Highway Patreon community. The feedback has been great for all who have joined. title: Episode 213: A MotherвҖҷs Day Episode: 10 Ways to Fill Your Cup in 5 Minutes or Less
summary: This week on the podcast, weвҖҷre celebrating you, mama. рҹ’ӣ In this special MotherвҖҷs Day episode, we are holding space for the invisible load so many mothers carry and sharing 10 powerful self-care ideas that take five minutes or less. Because taking care of yourself doesnвҖҷt need to mean a full spa day (although thatвҖҷs lovely too!). Sometimes, itвҖҷs a few deep breaths, a warm cup of tea, or stepping outside to feel the sunshine. Whether you're packing lunchboxes, managing appointments, calming meltdowns, or remembering when the library books are dueвҖ”you are doing so much and you are recognised. This episode is a gentle reminder that your wellbeing matters and it doesnвҖҷt have to wait. HereвҖҷs what we touch on: A gentle reminder of the visible and invisible labour mothers carry every day.A special shoutout to mums managing their childrenвҖҷs health вҖ” from eczema and allergies to behavioral challenges.A mindset shift around self-care, so it no longer feels selfish (or unattainable).10 simple, powerful self-care ideas that take 5 minutes or less.рҹҺ§ Tune in for encouragement, practical tips, and the permission slip you didnвҖҷt know you needed. This episode is for every mum who needs a little reminder: youвҖҷre doing a phenomenal job and you deserve to feel good, too. Ready to try one of the 10 ideas Jessica shares? Start with one. title: Charlene Masterson : A Survivors Voice (re-release)

summary: Topics covered : Sexual abuse, grooming, domestic violence, rape, incest, sexual assault, coercive control, manipulation, gaslighting, guilt, shame, recovery. Charlene Masterson was subjected to horrific sexual abuse at the hands of her father, David, for over 7.5 years. The abuse started at 18 and the details of which are truly shocking, but her ability to talk about what she went through, is remarkable. This summer, she made the brave decision to waive her right to anonymity in the hope of helping others who find themselves in controlling, abusive situations. Now in her early 30вҖҷs she is getting back to what she loves to do and is finding herself again. And in this conversation we talk about so much, including the bond she has with her mother, the damage of pity and the importance of friendships. She is a gorgeous person, inside and out and deserves all the best that life can bring her. title: Why you aren't going to sort your life out and why that's a good thing with Oliver Burkeman
summary: Self-help books and advice gurus often promise big changes to revolutionise how you live your life, or push you to strive for perfection. But what if we accept the fact that we are mortal humans with a finite time on this Earth, with only so much ability to get things done - and if we place ourselves within that reality, is that when we can truly thrive?  This week, Francesca and Louise are talking about this philosophy with Oliver Burkeman, author of Meditations for Mortals, which explores why we are never going to sort our lives out, so it's time to work out how to succeed within our limitations.  A new version of Meditations for Mortals releases July 1st.В  See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: 4. A Long-haul Flight Every Single Day | Jonathan Tierney, Seating Matters
summary: "A long-haul flight is a strain on the body because an airline seat is not made to measure. Those of us who are able, can change position, get up from our seats and walk around to stretch. Many patients and residents in hospitals and care facilities are unable to do this without assistance. This means that for many, their seating experience is the equivalent of a long-haul flight and most have to do it day after day after day, living in pain and discomfort." *** My son Jonathan interviewed me earlier in the series and in this episode I have turned the tables and got to interview him about his role as Seating Matters Commercial Director.  Jonathan talks passionately of the human impact of being able to access suitable healthcare equipment and the reality of working with his two brothers and his mother!
